deconstruction_targets: mere composing or uttering of words as having intrinsic power

scapegoat.name: certain people
attributes: using charms for worms in limbs
pouring Holy Water into mouths of cattle
scapegoat.justification: These individuals are condemned as practitioners of unlawful, superstitious remedies that rely on words or actions without divine sanction, thereby violating proper religious and moral order.

moral_justification: The text asserts that only God-given sacramental words and public prayer have true efficacy, while all other verbal or magical acts are invalid due to their lack of divine institution and meaning-based power.
summary: This passage deconstructs folk remedies for hailstorms and bewitched animals by condemning superstitious practices as morally and theologically invalid, arguing that only divinely ordained rituals possess true power.
